//: The other half of the src/dashkit/ typecheck. tsconfig.json covers the DEPLOYED slice with types: [] so a stray Bun/node global is a compile error; that same barrier makes `bun:test` unresolvable. Test files are never bundled into the vault, so they keep Bun's types — and get DOM, because they assert against DOM-typed signatures. Both configs run under `bun run typecheck:dashkit`. COVERAGE, stated precisely: every .ts under src/dashkit/ is checked by AT LEAST ONE config, and every NON-TEST .ts is checked by the `types: []` config. index.ts is in fact checked twice — it enters here as an import of the test file, with Bun/node globals available — which is harmless only because both configs must pass and the strict one is what actually gates.
//exclude: Same inherited-exclude trap as the sibling config — root's ["src/cn/**", "src/dashkit/**"] would otherwise leave zero input files.
